acceptance_targets: [chapter_08_F004] PASS if each speaker (Lena, Mara, Clay, Cain) has at least one clearly distinct rhythm/lexicon marker in lines 185-235 and no three-turn sequence sounds interchangeable by syntax; FAIL if voices remain swappable.

[chapter_08_F005] PASS if revised lines 153-201 contain at least two interruption/overlap beats and high-contraction speakers in that span (e.g., Mara) use contractions in most eligible lines; FAIL if the exchange still reads as uninterrupted podium copy.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each speaker (Lena, Mara, Clay, Cain) has at least one clearly distinct rhythm/lexicon marker in lines 185-235 and no three-turn sequence sounds interchangeable by syntax; FAIL if voices remain swappable.
findings.problem: Register separation blurs in the town-hall exchange. Lena, Mara, Clay, and Cain repeatedly converge on the same policy-brief cadence, weakening idiolect separation required by the style bible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:185-235 so Mara's lines use fast interrogative pivots, Clay's lines retain evasive institutional framing, Cain's lines carry sermonic memory texture, and Lena alternates procedural precision with stress-shortened bursts.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines 153-201 contain at least two interruption/overlap beats and high-contraction speakers in that span (e.g., Mara) use contractions in most eligible lines; FAIL if the exchange still reads as uninterrupted podium copy.
findings.problem: Dialogue pressure drops into stilted official prose at key conflict peaks; multiple lines read like policy memo dictation rather than contested live speech, muting interruption and colloquial rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and roughen spoken delivery in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:153-201 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:309-314 by adding interruption beats, shorter turn collisions, and register-appropriate contractions where speakers are not intentionally performing public formality.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on dialogue realism and voice separation: idiolect fidelity, leverage shifts, pressure cadence, anti-transcript behavior, natural colloquial texture (contractions/interruptions/slang when character-true), and pressure-true profanity usage per aesthetic risk policy.
revision_pass.label: dialogue/idiolect/cadence fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
